title: Simulate a Senate Estimates Hearing
description: Simulate a Senate Estimates hearing — prepare as both the questioning senator and the responding minister/public servant. This is the ultimate test of political preparation, policy knowledge, and performance under pressure.
whyItMatters: Senate Estimates is where governments are held accountable for how they spend public money. It produces more political news stories than almost any other parliamentary process. Mastering Estimates — whether asking or answering — is a defining skill for any serious politician or public servant.

steps.title: Choose a portfolio
steps.description: Select a government portfolio (Health, Education, Defence, Home Affairs). Research the department's recent budget papers and annual report.
steps.tips: Start with a portfolio you know something about. The learning is in the process, not the topic.

steps.title: Prepare as questioner
steps.description: Draft 20 questions across three themes: budget expenditure, program performance, and policy implementation. Structure as opening, follow-up, and gotcha.
steps.tips: The best Estimates questions build a narrative — each answer reveals information that makes the next question more pointed.

steps.title: Prepare as respondent
steps.description: Anticipate the 20 hardest questions your portfolio might face. Draft responses that are accurate, concise, and don't volunteer unnecessary information.
steps.tips: Public servants answer factual questions. Ministers handle policy questions. Know the boundary.

steps.title: Run the simulation
steps.description: With a partner, run a 45-minute simulated Estimates hearing. One plays the senator, one plays the minister. Then swap roles.
steps.tips: Stay in character. The value is in the pressure of real-time interrogation.

steps.title: Debrief and refine
steps.description: Review what worked and what didn't. Which questions landed? Which responses were evasive? What would you do differently?
steps.tips: Record the simulation if possible. Watching yourself under pressure is brutally educational.

deliverables: A 20-question Estimates brief with supporting research
A defensive brief with anticipated questions and prepared responses
A recording or transcript of the simulated hearing
A debrief document with lessons learned

rubric.description: Are the questions specific, evidence-based, and strategically sequenced?
Are the responses accurate, appropriately concise, and politically aware?
Does the simulation follow realistic Estimates procedures?
Can participants maintain composure and clarity under adversarial questioning?
Does the debrief demonstrate genuine learning about budget scrutiny and accountability?
